guardrail: static projection of the public register; no facts added here

trigger_kind_legend.purpose: Every event carries a trigger_kind: the paper's typology of regulatory triggers by the kind of certainty each carries (flagship §3). It separates the one kind that moves a dated horizon from the kinds that do not, and names the intra-regime gating change a class-centric reading misses.

kinds.fully-scheduled: gazetted commencement date; applied by that date; the ONLY kind that moves a horizon (§3.1, UK).
kinds.enacted-not-commenced: passed into law but awaiting subsidiary legislation with no gazetted commencement; partly fired (§3.3, TW).
kinds.contingent-no-date: depends on an enactment that has not occurred; no date; surfaced only as an if-enacted branch (§3.2 US, §3.4 KR).
kinds.contingent-not-class-change: a pending enactment that hardens a binding status without moving any class, the baseline class already in force (§3.5, SG).
kinds.parameterization: operationalization of a constraint that already binds; sharpens the operative content of a class without moving one (§3.7, EBA, carried in open_questions).
kinds.intra-regime-gating: a DATED change in law that alters accessibility WITHIN a live regime (who may operate; which tokens are admissible) without moving any feasibility class (§3.10, EU MiCA).
kinds.dated-empty-effect: a dated, now-in-force change with an explicitly empty feasibility-class effect; the control case showing the calendar distinguishes a change in law from a change in feasibility class (§3.6, JP).
kinds.inbound-recognition: a dated, now-in-force change in law that opens an inbound recognition or equivalence channel for qualifying foreign instruments; its reach is set by the recognition criteria and is handled by conditioning (the existing Category II channelled treatment), so it asserts no new class (§3.6 second same-day change; §5.3 dated recognition instance, JP).
kinds.open-compositional: information rather than a change in law, whose resolution feeds another jurisdiction's analysis (§3.8, HK 2nd cohort, carried in open_questions).
kinds.resolved-tightening: an asymmetric posture: a boundary's recent movement is settled in one direction while a reversing development stays open (§3.9, PRC, carried in open_questions).

trigger_kind_legend.horizon_rule: Only fully-scheduled events move a compose() horizon. dated-empty-effect, intra-regime-gating, and inbound-recognition events are dated but move no feasibility class, so they appear on the timeline without changing any corridor's category, by design.

data.title: UK systemic stablecoin regime + FCA conduct regime operative (SI 2026/102 full commencement)
data.basis: FSMA 2026 framework via SI 2026/102: made 4 Feb 2026; the BoE systemic-stablecoin regime and FCA conduct regime become operative on full commencement 25 Oct 2027.
data.date_basis: Gazetted commencement DAY: SI 2026/102 (FSMA 2000 (Cryptoassets) Regulations 2026) made 4 Feb 2026, full commencement 25 Oct 2027 (preparatory provisions from 25 Feb 2026). precision=day; a specific commencement date, not a modelled year.
data.source_note: effective_date corrected 2027-01-01 (modelled year) -> 2027-10-25 (gazetted commencement day) per the v0.9.5 external verification of SI 2026/102.

data.title: Taiwan Virtual Asset Service Act commencement (subsidiary legislation brings the issuer regime into force)
data.trigger: Commencement of the enacted Virtual Asset Service Act through its subsidiary legislation. The Act passed third reading on 30 Jun 2026; commencement awaits FSC subordinate rules and carries no gazetted date.
data.basis: Virtual Asset Service Act enacted at third reading 30 Jun 2026; tw-frs-issuer_pathway-001 carries the enacted provisions as status=transitional (binding_status=made_not_commenced), awaiting FSC subsidiary legislation.
data.date_basis: Enacted at third reading on 30 Jun 2026, the dateline of this draft; commencement awaits subsidiary legislation (the FSC has flagged roughly nine subordinate items on an indicative timetable around Q1 2027), and no commencement date is gazetted, so this trigger is applied by no date. The Legislative Yuan Finance Committee completed the article-by-article first review (初審) on 3 Jun 2026 and resolved to send the bill to the plenary; third reading followed on 30 Jun 2026. On commencement the inbound gate moves to a channelled Category II route; the origin edges stay Category III, because enactment alone creates no exportable, comprehensively authorizable private token (issuers must still be licensed and issue under the new regime). Procedurally now ahead of KR's DABA, still in subcommittee.

data.title: Korea Digital Asset Basic Act enacted (won-stablecoin issuer regime live)
data.trigger: Enactment and commencement of the Digital Asset Basic Act (currently a bill).
data.basis: Draft Digital Asset Basic Act; kr-frs-issuer_pathway-001 carries the draft eligibility question as status=proposed.
data.date_basis: No firm enactment date. As of late Apr 2026 the Digital Asset Basic Act and three stablecoin-specific bills remained in the National Assembly Political Affairs Committee subcommittee (정무위 소위), with a full-committee session around mid-May 2026; an H2-2026 passage goal is described as uncertain, delayed by the won-stablecoin issuer-eligibility (51%) dispute (the Bank of Korea favouring a bank-majority consortium) and the June 2026 local elections / committee reconstitution. Procedurally less advanced than TW's VAS Act (already past committee first review).

data.title: Japan Act No. 66 of 2025 full enforcement (trust-type reserve relaxation)
data.basis: Act No. 66 of 2025 relaxing trust-type reserve rules; refines an already-live EPI regime.
data.date_basis: Act No. 66 of 2025 promulgated 13 Jun 2025; took effect 1 Jun 2026 and is now in force as of the base date. Effect on compose() is NONE (control case): Japan is already 'live', so relaxing trust-type reserve composition moves no inbound gate and changes no edge class. Included to show the calendar distinguishes a change in law from a change in feasibility class. The same-date Cabinet Office Ordinance recognising equivalent foreign trust-type stablecoins is now recorded as its own first-class event (jp-foreign-stablecoin-recognition), not folded here.

data.title: Japan recognises equivalent foreign trust-type stablecoins as Electronic Payment Instruments (Cabinet Office Ordinance)
data.basis: Cabinet Office Ordinance effective 1 Jun 2026 recognising foreign trust-type stablecoins judged equivalent to the Japanese regime as electronic payment instruments, admitted through a registered EPIESP; opens an inbound recognition channel whose reach is set by the equivalence criteria.
data.date_basis: Effective 1 Jun 2026 (same date as Act No. 66 full enforcement, but a distinct change with a different character). This is the second same-day change the paper separates at §3.6: not a domestic reserve adjustment but an inbound-side development that opens a recognition pathway for qualifying foreign trust-type stablecoins. Effect on edge class is handled by CONDITIONING, not asserted: a recognised foreign token reaches Japan through the recognition-and-registration channel the directed model already scores as Category II (channelled), and the operative reach of that channel turns on the equivalence determination (the open variable). No new class is asserted on its account beyond the existing Category II channelled treatment of inbound-to-Japan edges. This is the dated, concrete instance §5.3 refers to: recognition and equivalence functioning as the interoperability protocol of the regulatory layer, dated to a specific instrument on a specific day.

data.title: US CLARITY Act enacted (digital-asset market structure; intermediary yield + routing layer live)
data.trigger: Enactment of the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act (H.R. 3633). On the Senate Legislative Calendar as Calendar No. 423 (reported 1 Jun 2026); still requires Senate floor passage, reconciliation with the Senate Agriculture and House versions, and Presidential signature.
data.basis: GENIUS is already in force (the issuer-level layer). CLARITY is the pending market-structure layer (H.R. 3633): us-pss-bank_nonbank_routing-001 binds on it, and the us-pss-permitted_activity_yield-001 / us-pss-securities_classification-001 / us-pss-issuer_pathway-001 cells carry its Sec. 404 and market-structure parts as flagged pending overlays.
data.date_basis: No firm enactment date. 2026 passage is roughly even (Polymarket near 48% in late Jun 2026, down from about 74% a month earlier); the practical window requires Senate floor passage before the August recess. Modelled as contingent until a date is fixed.

data.title: Singapore SCS framework implementing legislation enacted (MAS-regulated stablecoin regime live)
data.trigger: Enactment of the implementing legislation for the MAS Single-Currency Stablecoin (SCS) framework (finalised 15 Aug 2023). MAS announced at the Nov 2025 Singapore FinTech Festival that it was ready to begin drafting; the framework is expected to take effect in mid-2026.
data.basis: The PS Act 2019 DPT baseline is already in force. The SCS-specific framework is finalised MAS policy pending implementing legislation; the sg-scs-* requirement cells carry it as binding_status=finalized_policy_pending, status=transitional.
data.date_basis: No firm enactment date. MAS guidance points to a mid-2026 effective date; drafting was announced ready as of Nov 2025. Modelled as contingent until a date is fixed.

data.title: EU MiCA Article 143(3) transitional grandfathering expiry (national side-doors close; admissible-token set narrows)
data.basis: MiCA Article 143(3) (Reg. (EU) 2023/1114) transitional grandfathering for CASPs providing services under pre-existing national law. eu-emt-regulatory_authority-001 carries the corrected staggered framing; this event carries the dated national schedule structurally so compose()/timeline can reflect it.
data.date_basis: 1 July 2026 is the OUTER CAP: the longest window any Member State could grant under Article 143(3); ESMA has confirmed no extension. Beneath the cap the national windows are staggered and most have already closed: France, Malta and Luxembourg run the full window (to 1 July 2026); Germany and Ireland closed at 12 months (end 2025); Sweden at 9 months (late 2025); Finland, Latvia, Lithuania, Hungary, the Netherlands, Poland and Slovenia at 6 months (mid-2025). The deadline that binds a given corridor is the EARLIEST among the Member States it touches, not the cap. effective_date is set to the outer cap for the timeline; because the effect on feasibility class is empty (intra-regime gating, not a class transition), no compose() horizon shifts on this date.

second_order.development: expected secondary authorization for the custody and transfer of electronic-money tokens (EMTs)
second_order.status: open, no commencement to apply by date
second_order.note: recorded alongside the transitional expiry as the related second-order EU development; bears on Interaction Set D (reserve composition jointly binding with monetary sovereignty), not on any feasibility class.

data.scope_note: Intra-regime gating: the directed model's feasibility classes capture whether a corridor can be authorized at all and by construction do not capture a change that alters WHO may operate within an already-live regime. This event closes national side-doors and narrows the set of admissible tokens without moving any class; the flagship treats the class-sensitivity measure's blind spot to this category as a stated scope limitation (§6).
